title: Schilleria lineata
intro: Schilleria lineata, also known as Schilleria lineata or Khas or Khaskhas, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Schilleria lineata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Schilleria lineata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its seeds are also used to make a traditional cough syrup.
taxonomy: Schilleria lineata belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae and is classified as an endangered species. It is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Africa. The classification of Schilleria lineata is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Malpighiales. This plant is also known as the "stiltroot" or "Schilleria stiltroot". The classification of Schilleria lineata is still being researched and refined by botanists.
